skill.name: Tool UI Components
skill.description: Tool lifecycle UI components for React and Next.js applications. Display tool call states including pending, running, approval required, success, and error. Provides components for showing agent tool calls, human-in-the-loop approvals, and tool output results.
skill.summary: React UI components for displaying AI agent tool lifecycle states with approval flows
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md). No executable code detected. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 issues. This skill provides UI component documentation for displaying agent tool lifecycle states - purely informational content with no security risk.

content.user_title: Build Tool UI for AI Agents
content.value_statement: Add professional tool lifecycle UI to your React apps. Display pending, running, approval required, and completed states for AI agent tool calls with built-in human-in-the-loop approval flows.
actual_capabilities: Display tool call status: pending, running, approval required, success, error
Show tool arguments and results in formatted UI cards
Provide approve/deny buttons for human-in-the-loop workflows
Auto-generate icons based on tool name patterns
Integrate with Agent component for automatic lifecycle handling
limitations: Requires React or Next.js project - not compatible with other frameworks
Installation requires npx and shadcn CLI tools
Components are part of ui.inference.sh registry - requires internet to fetch
Does not provide backend tool execution - only UI display

use_cases.title: Build AI Agent Interfaces
use_cases.description: Create chat interfaces that display what tools the AI agent is using, showing real-time status updates as tools execute.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building AI chatbot applications

use_cases.title: Implement Approval Workflows
use_cases.description: Add human approval gates before dangerous tool executions like sending emails or deleting files.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developers requiring audit trails for AI actions

use_cases.title: Debug AI Tool Calls
use_cases.description: Visualize tool arguments and results during development to understand how AI agents are using tools.

prompt_templates.title: Add Tool UI Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Add tool UI components to my Next.js project using the inference.sh registry. I want to display tool calls with status indicators.

prompt_templates.title: Show Tool Approval Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a tool approval component that shows pending tool calls with approve and deny buttons. The tool should show the tool name and arguments.

prompt_templates.title: Display Tool Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to display tool execution results using the ToolResult component. I want to show success or error states.

prompt_templates.title: Style Tool Cards
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I customize the styling of tool call cards? I want to add custom border colors and modify the appearance based on tool status.

output_examples.input: Add tool UI components to my project
output_examples.output:
Run: npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.inference.sh/r/tools.json

This adds ToolCall, ToolResult, and ToolApproval components to your registry.

<ToolCall
  name="search_web"
  args={{ query: "latest AI news" }}
  status="running"
/>

<ToolApproval
  name="send_email"
  args={{ to: "user@example.com", subject: "Hello" }}
  onApprove={() => executeTool()}
  onDeny={() => cancelTool()}
/>

best_practices: Use the Agent component for automatic tool lifecycle handling instead of manual state management
Enable approval flows for tools that modify external state (email, file operations, API calls)
Use consistent status indicators across your application for better user experience
anti_patterns: Do not skip approval flows for destructive operations like delete or send actions
Avoid hardcoding tool names - pass them dynamically from your agent configuration
Do not ignore error states - always display error information to users

faq.question: What tools are compatible with these components?
faq.answer: These components work with any tool that follows standard tool call patterns. They display tool name, arguments, and status regardless of the underlying tool implementation.

faq.question: Do I need a backend to use these components?
faq.answer: The components are purely frontend. You need your own backend to execute tools, but the UI will display whatever status you pass to it.

faq.question: Can I use these with Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, these components are tool-agnostic. You can use them with Claude, Codex, or any other AI that makes tool calls.

faq.question: How do I handle tool errors?
faq.answer: Pass status="error" to ToolResult component along with the error message in the result object.

faq.question: Is this free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, these components are open source under MIT license from ui.inference.sh.

faq.question: Do I need shadcn/ui to use these?
faq.answer: Yes, these components are distributed through the shadcn registry. You need a Next.js project with shadcn configured.
